reviews.review_text:
I've finished this game months ago, and I usually always write a review after my first playthrough or 100%ing the game. I bought this game for dirt cheap and back then, I had 0 idea what DOOM was about. As someone who appreciates slow paced and story-driven games, DOOM eternal flipped a switch in me I never knew I had. I remember being so freaking "scared" and stressed out by this game, I literally had to force myself to enjoy it. I've since then adapted to the entire system and knew when to do what and that's when you actually start loving this game. Everything from the design choice to the absolute PEAK of metal music, this game really is the embodiment of pure testosterone and adrenaline. This game probably has the smoothest gameplay flow I've ever seen. The DOOM Slayer is a self-sustaining GOD in this game and I really can't exaggerate that enough. I don't really know why I took my sweet time to write this review, but I just knew this game deserved a second chance, so I gave it and now I'm in loooove. The only thing that'll actually get you fuming is the Marauder, believe me, it takes me way too long to actually kill that thing. Regarding the online multiplayer mode, it's completely dead but who gives a ♥♥♥♥, right? After years of hearing compliments and seeing people worship this game, I finally know why they do so.

𝕽𝕴𝕻 𝕬𝕹𝕯 𝕿𝕰𝕬𝕽!
This is easily my favourite game of all time . Go and shoot whatever you see , as simple as that . Worth it

one of the most addictive games ever words can not express the love i have for this game. I've played it from my ps4, Xbox and now pc. Best doom game in my opinion can't wait to buy a graphics card to play dark ages

Hell doesn’t scare me anymore.
It schedules appointments.

In DOOM Eternal, you don’t enter combat — you’re launched into it like a blunt instrument with legs.
There’s no time to think, no time to breathe, and definitely no time to reload your emotions.

Every arena is a violent puzzle.
Shoot this demon with that gun, rip ammo from this one, burn that one for armor, dash, jump, glory kill, repeat.
You’re not fighting Hell — you’re managing chaos at 200 BPM.

The Slayer doesn’t talk.
He doesn’t need to.
His entire personality is “enough is enough,” and somehow that silence carries more weight than a thousand speeches about destiny.

The soundtrack doesn’t accompany you — it possesses you.
At some point, you stop playing the game and start moving to the rhythm of controlled violence.
It’s beautiful. In a “this might be unhealthy” way.

• Entered an arena → Immediately overwhelmed.
• Found my flow → Became a weapon.
• Still pushing forward → Because stopping means thinking, and thinking is dangerous.

DOOM Eternal is precision + rage + momentum.
If you love this game… congratulations, your brain now measures fun in demon corpses per minute.

9.5/10 – Came to kill demons.
Stayed because Hell blinked first.

Insane game. Insane design. Insane combat. Insane everything. Love it. Top to bottom. And the soundtrack just cannot be better. Heck, it's the reason I got to know about this game. Thank you Mick Gordon, thank you Bethesda and thank you id (screw you Marty, you ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥).

Only thing I could call an issue here is the fact that you can't link your progress and cosmetics between PlayStation and Steam but that's literally the only thing that I don't like here. Thank you. Love and appreciate you all.

Rip and tear. Until it is done.

DOOM Eternal feels like finding a bowl of your favorite candy that has been laced with pure human adrenaline. The pace feels great, the controls feel smooth and responsive, and the game-play loop is so very satisfying when it finally clicked in my ADHD riddled mind. It felt strange at first but before long I was jumping around arenas, juggling weapons and abilities and mowing down an onslaught of demons that use everything they have to bring me down.

Managing rescores by using certain attacks feels amazing and constantly awards you with hits of dopamine. Need ammo? Chainsaw time. Need health? glory kill away. Then setting some demons on fire so they drop armor and on top of that each demon has a weakness to exploit when you aren't trying to regain lost health or ammo. It just plain feels good.

The story is pretty good too, though purposely campy, placing a greater emphasis on the series' lore than previous entries, exploring the Slayer's origins and the mythology surrounding his endless hatred for the demons, while maintaining the fast-paced action that define the franchise.
They force you to create a Bethesda account. It's a single-player game for goodness' sake.

Is there a reason why a 10yr old game is priced at £35 (std edition), or is this just typical Bethesda greed? There is at most 16hrs play time, so only ever buy this when it its on sale for £10 and only if its the full version that includes ALL the DLC.

The game is not bad. It's a solid 7/10 game. I just make it red to highlight several issues. If you have never played doom, this is not the game to unwind yourself. It is very fast and expect real effort and reflexes  from the player.  If you come from Doom 2016 it plays different, even faster and enemies more aggressive than the previous game. 

good things first
+buttery smooth doom gameplay
+great visuals, art direction, immaculate environments jampacked with details
+should run very well on mid range GPUs
+it is a very long game just to beat the campaign
+a ton of collectibles and the game makes it easier to complete them when u clear it
+falling is not death anymore

Bad things
-the game starts out poorly. Your doomguy starts super weak and the game make you fight big enemies in small spaces early and gives you the tools much later after you have suffered enough
-every tool that makes doomguy strong comes late and the game becomes engaging on second half. 
-Not all weapon mods, upgrades and abilities are equal. some will make your life much easier and almost essential to get it first. If you pick ♥♥♥♥♥♥ ones by mistake, early game is torment.  
-Arena design is inconsistent. Some are badly littered with debris to get stuck on and too much obstacles and hazards.
-you will encounter some software bugs

Questionable design choices
-you need to run in circles to survive the arena and fight when opportunity presents while you run. 
-enemies are immune to environmental hazards except red barrels. Why?
-enemies are immune to each other's attacks. 
-Enemies are very accurate in both range and melee and tend to body block you. Even small demons can body block and corner. 

Some tips, stop reading if you want to experience on your own
[spoiler]
+choose sniper scope on heavy canon machine gun first. It is needed to hit weak spots. weak spots only react fast to the sniper mode. You face big enemies early in tight spaces. 
+choose grenade launcher first on combat shotgun
+ice grenade is better than frag
+fire belch is your best friend and make it cool down fast ASAP. Arenas are small and you are supposed to tank hits with it. There is no way to dodge all devastating hits. 
+super shotgun is another best friend. Get the mastery as soon as possible. 
+chainsaw only the filler demons
+there is one demon which needs to be searched taken out with highest priority with your most powerful weapon/ability. Save one charge of your trump card for this guy. 
+upgrade cooldowns and speed related abilities as fast as possible.
[/spoiler]

I installed this game thinking I’d casually play for 30 minutes. 14 hours later I was sprinting around my room mentally chain sawing furniture for ammo. 100/10!!! This game cured my fear of public speaking because after fighting a Marauder... nothing scares me anymore!
Best game and for higher graphics cards the ray tracing is insane
More big guns, and even bigger demons to take down with those guns.
I LOVE to Rip and Tear through pure evil on my holy crusades, even as an atheist, thanks to this game. Thanks id!

My first time playing Doom was around 2002/03, that's when I realized how much I like the Doom saga, years later I'm still playing, I'm still here!! The new DOOM games are wonderful, the gameplay is decent, the music, the environments... it's truly a highly recommended game if you like massacring everything in your path.

everything is peak in this game except for samuel hayden because the longer the icon of sin is on the earth the stronger he will become

Anyways props to Mick Gordon for the amazing music in the base campaign, it's a shame he didn't make the music in the dlcs

i played this game before playing DOOM 2016, it was a blast and a ton of fun
i replayed it after dark ages and doom 16, and it is still unique and very delivering of the doom experience.
that said, the second DLC is not great at all, it has very bad new demons introduced with not fun to play againist mechanics and the weakest boss fights in the series as a whole.
the base game can be rated 10/10 easily, and the second dlc can take like 6 or 5/10 
i recommend skipping the dlcs for this game and maybe if you want to know what happened see a quick recap on youtube, so you dont miss on the story part
Легенда игр с очень харизматичным гг о котом никогда не забуду, и мне кажется что каждый мужик должен попробовать в это поиграть
easily the most polished fps game i've ever played, the gameplay feels amazing and its totally worth what i spent

+200h on xbox..

this game is the BEST game of 2020 and deserved GOTY. literally the only downside of doom eternal is the fact the actually fun pvp is just dead. i love you bethesda and i esp LOVE my handsome sexy goat mick gordon you ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ saint drenched in heavenly ears.

This game is absolutely incredible—my favorite FPS of all time, and one of the best games ever made.

The gameplay is pure perfection; playing it puts you in a trance-like state. It may seem like a brainless shooter at first, but it’s actually quite the opposite. You constantly have to think and react quickly, adapting to every situation. Every weapon has its use, and you have to swap between them often—there’s never a moment to stop, and the arenas are perfectly designed to emphasize that.

The level design is great. I did have a preference for Doom 2016’s more labyrinthine levels, but Eternal’s scenery are stunning and much more varied.

The music is headbang-inducingly cool and a key part of the transcendental experience this game delivers.

The story isn’t the main focus, but if you’re a longtime fan of the franchise, it has a lot to offer and will definitely hype you up.

My only complaint would be that the default difficulty doesn’t let you fully experience the game’s potential, and you have to play at least on Ultra Violence to actually get a sense of its depth.

A true masterpiece of the FPS genre.

Doom eternal changed so much about the doom formula. Some don't like it but the game design is so robust. Time to kill enemies is a lot higher and ammo capacity is lower so you have to use the gun modifiers to get kills efficiently. There is a combo of like 5 mechanics like a freeze, flamethrower, blood punch, and chain saw at all times. You almost never need to spend time looking for ammo health and shield. It centralizes play and shrinks the sandbox a bit but there is a very strong very unique game play loop. They also drastically changed the movement aspect there is 2 dashes, a double jump, and a grapple hook on the super shotgun. So unlike doom 2016 you spend very little time on the ground and the new arena designs compliment the new movement perfectly. The Crusible is also like a extra destructive shot that can cheese almost any enemy and a nice addition. 

Its insane how much the game communicates with visuals and sound the game would not play the same if you didnt get the ♥♥♥♥♥ noises from breaking weak points, the blue glow for glory kills, and the alarm for low health. Visually they added even more enemies from the past and even more glory kills for each one. The enemies are so animated and have a lot of personaility. The games bossfights are pretty good and I think the enemies have good balance up for the main game and ancient gods 1. 

The soundtrack is still amazing and completely new tracks from that of 2016. Some highlights like "The only thing they fear is you" and "meat hook". They also brought back "BFG division" from 2016.
amazing game 9.99 / 10, but pinkys shaking my entire screen made me want to taste the cold steel of a shotgun

the main game is good  but the dlc's are the same thing over and over . they like to put a archvile at the end of a hallway and you have to get to him before he spawns a hoard of demons that you can not win against. all the other fights are just waves of tougher demons in the same order as the last fight , get it on sale, but pass on dlc's
It's a fun game but it was at least for me super difficult I spent hours on the campaign and still haven't beaten it once but great game, great graphics, worth the money 9/10.

An incredible and frenetic game. My Steam playtime doesn't reflect how much I've played it; I played it more on console (Xbox Series X) back in the day. However, the PC version is far superior.
For me, it's even better than "The Dark Ages," and I think that's a very good game too.

Este juego es una las experiencias mas peak en gaming que haya tenido en mi vida, es una inyeccion de dopamina directo a tu torrente sanguineo, con un sentimiento de adrenalina y frenetismo que pocos juegos me han hecho sentir.

Es un SUPER upgrade del 2016, y el moveset, herramientas, encuentros y cantidad de violencia, longitud de juego es *chefs kiss*.

Que si lo recomiendo? absolutamente.
really great game. not as good as the first one, but really good. also, you need an account for this game, just a disclaimer
Not as good as Doom 2016 but still a great addition to the franchise. Play 2016 for the traditional Doom experience, whereas Eternal has a more "arcade-y" feel.

One of the best standalone shooters of the 21st century. You will rarely find low moments as almost every minute of this game finds you mowing down massive hordes of demons in such a precise and stylish way that just really embodies the one man unstoppable army idea that should come with every doom game.
i played this game and it changed my life.this game and cod bo2 gave me my love for trolling and ragebaiting people and made me really like fps and puzzle games

Great game. People that say its too hard or frustrating just don't understand the game. When I first started, it was hard due to the fact that I was used to Doom 2016. At the start you don't get much and you can no longer punch to kill/damage for glory kill, it only staggers them. If you dodge/boost into them, it does damage, very minor of course, but it will allow glory kill after a while. The whole point of the gameplay is to use everything they give you throughout the game in order to win. You cannot just focus on healing or attacking, you have to do both. You get a chainsaw in the beginning that you MUST use in order to get ammo. The difference between 2016 and eternal with the chainsaw is that the fuel refills up to 1/3 automatically, regardless of getting a jerry can from the ground. In 2016, you would find ammo on ground more often. This is supposed to inspire the player to constantly get ammo and swap weapons. Swapping weapons is also forced upon the player for weak points on demons to bring them down quicker as well as using certain mods for weapons on certain demons. You use flame belch on large groups of enemies and use blood plunch or AoE weapons like the rocket launcher to gain lots of armor and perform glory kills for more health or combine the two. Additionally, using the super shotgun with firery meat hook to essentially flame belch one target and shoot them when close enough to gain armor and move around the map more is another must use. Moving around the map is also important for staying alive. Try out different combos of weapons in certain situations to overcome your enemies. You cannot run as fast like in doom 2016, so you gotta use your dodge/boost and jumping with meat hook on super shotgun to get around quickly to other obstacles that you can use to jump. Try to train demons like you would in zombies to take 'em out if possible. You have flame belch, glory kills, chainsaw for ammo, 2 dodges, 2 jumps, and a ton of weapons. Use all of em all the time. If weapon swapping is slow for you, just move around and again, GET HEALTH as you are fighting. TRY to stay topped off on ammo, health and armor at all times. Focus on bigger enemies, especially ones that can spawn in other enemies, while also using the small guys for ammo, health and armor. You can use flame belch with grenades as well in order to lessen amount of demons, clear a path, gain breathing room, and replenish. Freeze grenade is helpful when trying to take down larger enemies or simply get breathing room to get to a safer location. Hope this helps new or newer players looking to enjoy the game instead of being frustrated cause they dont understand. Again, I was same way at start and now I have beaten it on ultra-violence for first playthrough on campaign, TAG 1 and TAG 2. Love the music too!
I thought it was a pretty cool game. It was pretty difficult at some times because I chose hard for my first playthough but It was still fun.

DOOM Eternal is fricking amazing. The game has beautiful graphics, almost better than most games nowadays, consistently engaging combat, and a spectacular story. The combat takes a huge step up from 2016, as it consists more of fast paced action instead of the approach that 2016 took. Along with that, the story is great, really entertaining, and the collectibles that you can get from the campaign is great. Everything is fun, every step feels like a huge step of progression, and every collectible makes you feel satisfied. You feel like an unstoppable force of nature all the time, killing everything you can see with a variety of mechanics and weapons that make it even more fun. I recommend this game to every DOOM fan, or people who love first person shooters. 10/10

Oh did I forget to mention the soundtrack? ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ amazing
The best doom game of the new generation, best enemies best guns and best movement

Compared to DOOM 2016, Eternal feels more forgiving at the start but the game quickly reveals it has far more mechanics to juggle simultaneously. The ceiling on skill expression is genuinely higher.
My one real frustration is the Crucible. In practice it's so scarce throughout the campaign that it never becomes a real part of your toolkit. It doesn't fill the niche the BFG does. The mechanic was deleted and replaced in the DLCs entirely.
Visually, the game looks good and runs pretty well for what it is.

The best Doom game so far and definitely one of the best boomer-shooters you can play

Doom Eternal was a great evolution from Doom 2016. Run and gun your way through armies of demons! They definitely took the difficulty of Doom 2016 and cranked it up a couple notches with this one. If you enjoyed Doom 2016, the old school Doom games, or run n gun boomer shooters in general, you're going to enjoy this. Doom Eternal ran really well on my Steam Deck. I feel like this game goes on sale all the time, so wait for the next sale and grab a copy.
Really fun movement and combat system, I thought that the DLC's were on the weaker side, but definitely add cool aspects to the game.
Fantastic old school shooter. Nothing else to say really. All around higly recommended.
great game all the doom games are even though i played this fully and beat it on easiest gamemode but everyone starts some where its my first full playthrough but i love it
One of the most polished and frenetic gameplays I've ever seen, totally recommended if you like brutal action that gives you a feeling of power while keeping you on your toes!

Been playing on and off for a couple years, definitely always has me coming back. I dont wanna start doom  the dark ages til i beat this and all the DLC so its just taunting me for now lol. love eternal tho

I came directly after beating Doom 2016 for the first time, and this one is even better! Its such fast paced adrenaline filled action, I absolutely love it. It runs just as well as the prequel did, I get a stable 70fps on ultra even with my rig. What a banger, highly recommend it!

[h1] 10/10 [/h1]
Cannot wait to play Doom: The Dark Ages after only playing 2 campaign levels on a respectable difficulty (I'm not bad, just long levels, which is good!)
It's crazy that this absolute masterpiece of a soundtrack comes with a free game about killing demons.

Have played it twice before buying it here, one of the best games of all time, doesn't get old.
Battlemode is also pretty fun if you get the hang of it.
But playing Doom 2016 first is mandatory if you want to enjoy it as well.

As Gman Lives said, this is an Elevation of the Shooter Genre. Played 200+ Hours during COVID. So much fun, and weapon switching tech is crazy. Still haven't Finished Ultra-Nightmare, but I've played Nightmare so many times.

its perfect, i love the glory kills, the textures are great, the soundtrack is amazing,
you should buy this game

avg FPS with Ultra settings without raytracing is 120 FPS with my specs

What is there to say?  It's Doom....fast, violent, brutal, long, challenging, and Mick Gordon!  Honestly, I am a FPS addict and every game I come across I end up comparing it to a Doom entry and they all fail.  It's Doom.  Nuff said.

Probably one of the best FPS games of all time. My only complaint is that they really shafted Mick Gordon. Very cringe!

10/10 game but 0/10 company.

Wanna feel powerful? Play doom eternal. Your moveset is incredibly large, allowing for some very stylish plays for each weapon and ability. The demons are all very different from each other so the challenge is still there even after many hours playing. Also offers some balanced difficulties if you are a casual, while some painful ones if you are up for a challenge.

As someone who doesnt usually like shooters, and is overly bad at them. 

This is the greatest game of all time. It is the pinnacle of human creation. If we were going to meet a new species of life, this game should be the first thing we show them.
very good game and the story is kinda peak. But its little expensive

One of my favorite games from the series, in love with the meathook, and as always, god bless Mick Gordon

this is without a doubt the best fps game i have ever played in my life, everything flows and encourages you to go fast, many people complain about this game being bad and arcadey, those people usually play this as if it was doom 2016 where you could just choose one weapon and go with it, that had things like siege mode gauss cannon, double tap super shotgun or remote detonation rocket launcher making the game a joke, as well as ammunition being abundant, doom eternal in contrast forces you to use your arsenal to its fullest potential and makes you be as aggressive as possible, ammunition, health and armour are all gained through killing demons, giving this game an unparalelled flow and joy, getting good at doom eternal is one of the most fun experiences in gaming

8.5/10. DOOM Eternal's combat system is amazing. It is so incredibly fast-paced and momentum based that all I wanted to do is slay more and more and more demons. I think DOOM Eternal is best played on the highest difficulty in my opinion, that really makes the combat system shine. However the story is only fine, it's definitely not as amazing as it's predecessor DOOM, and to me I always just wanted to get to the next combat arena, which the story took me away from sometimes.

So, gameplay wise I muuuuuch prefer DOOM Eternal over DOOM, but this game is a little wonky on the story department.

One of the most fun games I've ever played. I only played it before Doom 2016 because it downloaded first and I regret it because I know I won't like 2016 as much now that I've played this alleged greatly improved sequel.
Best FPS game ever made as far as mechanics and overall gameplay feel are concerned.

Best FPS ever made. This is second time buying this game after owning it on PS4.
No replay every gets boring.
peak doom gameplay, the vibes is not as good as 2016 tho plus justice for mick gordon

I was a massive fan of 2016, but could never get into Eternal because i thought it was too much weapon swapping and tutorial.  I gave it a go again for the 3rd time and really made an attempt to learn the game systems,  I can say now that "I get it".  Im really starting to have a great time with the game.  There are still some small things about 2016 i miss, but Eternal shines in its own right.  Im looking forward to playing Dark Ages as well when im done.
sooo ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ goood. This is what peak gaming looks like. ♥♥♥♥ and ball torture but enjoyable. Recommended to play at a higher difficulty and master the doom dance.
played the game for a long time before this on xbox still good to this day even if your not good at these type of games. they put in codes to help people get through who are really struggling.

This DOOM game is one of the best gunplay wise, some say its the best FPS game out there. Unlike the other DOOM games, you can combo and chain your guns to do massive damage. Once mastered can be vary satisfying to slay hundreds of demons in seconds. the story is good and has the classic DOOM badass moments. The music is amazing and gets you in the seat of the slayer. Rip & Tear until its done!

Most of my time was spent exploring the vast world and trying to find collectables. I found this significantly harder and longer than Doom (2016) but still enjoyed the game thoroughly. The SuperShotgun Meathook introduced a fun, fast paced, thrilling experiance; Definetly my favorite part of the game. The mechanics were also fun to play around with.

The base game is a masterpiece. I'd go as far as to say that it's the best FPS ever made in terms of mechanics, pacing and the innovations it brought to the table. The Ancient Gods on the other hand is a pain to get through.

This is legitimately one of the most enjoyable shooters I have ever played. Ridiculously tight gameplay that feels like a natural evolution of classic boomshoots, taken up to the max. Enemies usually require a specific strategy, so you constantly find yourself switching weapons and movement, trying to keep the overall battle in mind, something I did not expect to enjoy, but I really do. 
People call Doom 2016 a power fantasy but honestly that same title can be applied to eternal, once you get a feel for the mechanics. This game forces you to do the things you would normally see in cutscenes and it somehow works absurdly well. Go play it.

I used to love this game when it first came out and played it to death.

Six years later and my tastes have changed, the game is too fast and much for me to process these days.

If you can handle a fast paced shooter, you will find a gem like no other.
very fun gameplay and interesting lore
Great game, much more faster paced than Doom 2016, it is kinda overwhelming at first but feels awesome when you get used to it. Also not to mention its extremely optimized and runs so well
peak game where you play as one of fictions most badass characters

English :

The best single-player FPS in recent years, with frantic gameplay, fun platforming sections, a stylish art direction, and incredible OSTs that make you want to tear through demons by the dozen. Not a single dull moment throughout the entire adventure. Thanks to additional difficulty levels, the replayability lets you dive even deeper into the depth and richness of the gameplay.

A fantastic game that runs on absolutely any PC thanks to perfect optimization. We're eagerly awaiting The Dark Age over here.

This rerun allowed me to discover the DLCs, which, although very short, give you a bit more time to enjoy the game!

P.S.: If you're a seasoned FPS player, play it on Nightmare mode—it makes the experience much more satisfying.

_____

Français :

Le meilleur FPS solo de ces dernières années, avec un gameplay frénétique, des phases de plateforme sympathiques, une direction artistique stylée et des OST incroyables qui donnent envie de défoncer des démons à la pelle. On ne s'ennuie pas une seconde tout au long de l'aventure. La rejouabilité, grâce aux difficultés supplémentaires, permet de profiter encore plus de la profondeur et de la richesse du gameplay.

Un jeu fantastique, qui tourne sur absolument tous les PC grâce à une optimisation parfaite. On attend The Dark Age avec impatience ici.

Ce rerun m'a permis de découvrir les DLC, qui, bien que très courts, permettent de passer un peu plus de temps en compagnie de ce jeu !

P.S : Si vous êtes un habitué des FPS, faites le jeu en mode cauchemardesque, cela le rend bien plus satisfaisant.

DOOM Eternal is an adrenaline-fueled blast of mayhem, with top-tier gamefeel, a godlike soundtrack, and a glorious arsenal that makes demon-slaying incredibly satisfying. It leans into old-school design with tight, fast-paced combat and creative level layouts. The story tries to matter, but honestly, it’s just background noise, and that’s fine.

Difficulty ramps up hard, especially near the end, and the massive arsenal can feel overwhelming in the heat of battle. It’s not the most elegant design, but when it works, it really works.

Brutal, intense, and unforgettable.

Doom Eternal is, without a doubt, my favorite first-person shooter of all time when it comes to gameplay. It's brutal, intense, gory and ridiculously fast-paced. You really feel like an unstoppable killing machine. Doom Guy is so simple minded and literally too angry to die, and the game does an incredible job of making you feel that rage and adrenaline as you tear through hordes of demons.

The gameplay is insanely well executed. You have dashes, double jumps, the super shotguns meat-hook, which lets you grapple through the air or launch yourself directly into demons, for a more close and personal destruction. There are lots of unique weapons and mods in your arsenal, and I do love how I actually find a use for all of them (excluding the Unmaykr). Everything flows perfect together. Need ammo? Chainsaw a fodder demon. Need health? Perform glory kills. speaking of glory kills, I absolutely love how much effort they put into them. With nearly 300 brutal animations, they perfectly showcase just how powerful and furious the Doom Slayer is, although I do wish the berserk power-ups had more unique ones, as it feels very limited in comparison.

Another huge part of what makes this game so incredible is the soundtrack, composed by Mick Gordon. The gritty, aggressive metal music fits the game perfectly and amplifies the intensity of every fight. The soundtrack constantly keeps your adrenaline pumping and makes every battle more chaotic and badass.

As for the story, while that's not why you play a Doom game, Eternal and The Ancient Gods DLCs do expand the lore quite a bit. The main plot revolves around Doom Slayer trying to eliminate hell's influence on earth by hunting down the three Hell Priests and stopping the demonic invasion. There's also the Khan Maykr, who made a deal with Hell to sacrifice worlds, including the life on earth, to create Argent Energy in order to sustain, and power her home world, Urdak. Doom Slayer takes it upon himself to stop all of it with his own hands. The story works well for a game primarily focused on action.

Even though it is my favorite FPS, the game isn't perfect. While the gameplay is, some of the bosses feels a bit underwhelming or poorly executed, especially the final boss who is basically just a bullet sponge. There are also occasional bugs, like the soundtrack not trigger properly or enemies failing to spawn, forcing you to restart from a checkpoint.

Still, Doom Eternal is just unbelievably fun to play. I've honestly lost count on how many times I've beaten it, yet I always find myself coming back for another playthrough just to get that adrenaline rush again. I highly recommend checking out all three of the modern Doom games, they are all worth playing, since each one feels unique while still being incredibly good in it's own way.

Worth it if you can get it at sale price.

DOOM Eternal cranks everything from the 2016 reboot up to absurd levels and somehow makes it work. The combat is faster, more aggressive, and demands you constantly juggle weapons, glory kills, chainsaw refills, and flame belch in this insane rhythm that becomes genuinely addictive once it clicks. Some people bounced off the platforming, and yeah, it's a divisive choice, but personally I loved how it kept the pacing relentless. The soundtrack is god-tier, the world expands the lore in wild directions, and ripping and tearing has never felt better. id Software at the top of their game.

The game is great overall, but the mechanics feel a little too childish and overly polished for something that’s supposed to be brutal like DOOM Eternal. It loses some of the raw, aggressive feeling that made the series so intense.

This game literally ruined all other First Person Shooters for me.  Anytime I pick up a new FPS I always end up comparing it to this one, and they all come up short (Including the latest addition to the Doom franchise).

This game starts off slow.  First, you're handed a pump shotgun with a few mods.  Then, you're given the heavy cannon with a precision mod that can destroy demon weakpoints.  Then, you're handed a grenade launcher that can stagger demons.  All of this happens in the first level.  Then, throughout the game, you're given more tools, more ways to express your skill and kick some demon ass in scenarios that would have seemed impossible to you a few hours prior.  The game flawlessly leads you down the path of mastery, and, by the end of the game, you're able to truly earn the title of DOOM SLAYER.

Also, this game runs at 400 FPS with Raytracing on.  Really puts the FUN in FUNctional.

There's a routine I engage in when I'm about to write a review. I play the game. Set it down for a few months (or in this case a few years) and then review it. The idea is to dodge the dopamine hit — the flashy core mechanic, the bait-and-switch graphics — and arrive at a verdict that has aged like a bottle of Domaine Leroy (1949). In the interim, I get to read ♥♥♥♥ takes in the reviews. Speaking of that...

If a reviewer (paid critic or otherwise) marks down a game for a specific enemy and can't tell you which mechanic makes that enemy have a bad design, they're not reviewing the enemy. They're ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ a couch and then complaining that it doesn't feel like the real thing. It's a self-own: they're writing about themselves and calling it a review. Their citation to the effects of the mob on [i]their[/i] gameplay, instead of the effects of the mob's mechanics on gameplay [i]in general[/i], is the dead giveaway that the opinion has no bearing on [b]you[/b].

This situation applies singularly to the Marauder in Doom Eternal. Critics on Steam and on Medium[dot]com (presented in order of competency) have, since release, been going full pygmy marmoset, flinging their excrement of inadequacy at this miniboss:

[i]He breaks the run-and-gun.[/i]
[i]His shield prevents me from W-keying the game.[/i]
[i]I have to adjust my combat to take him into account.[/i]

The thing to recognize here is that these aren't critiques of an enemy, they're a confession by projection of the player, and the confession is that they shouldn't be publishing reviews anywhere. One should welcome an enemy who forces an adjustment, because that adjustment is the entire mechanism by which a combat game avoids feeling stale and programmatic. The Marauder is the moment Doom Eternal stops being a power fantasy (which, and I'll fight you on this, the series never was) and starts being a challenge. Marking it down for that moment is marking down the genre for being the genre.

The irony is that the people marking the game down for the Marauder are missing that the Marauder [b][i]is[/i][/b] the game: he's a microcosm of the entire design philosophy.

The Marauder specifically, and Doom Eternal generally, demands you be on your A+ game 100% of the time. It is a fast game. It is a twitch game. It is a game that requires you to predict N=2 steps in advance. The keyboard is a Bösendorfer Imperial 290 and the game demands you play  Beethoven's Klaviersonate Nr. 14 in cis-Moll, op. 27, Nr. 2 "Sonata quasi una fantasia", Beethoven-Werkverzeichnis-equivalent, third movement marked Presto agitato. It is, therefore, a game that rewards hypersituational awareness, fast reflexes, and a questionable dependency on second-hand-market Adderall. 

This is what elevates the game from a boomer shooter to Doom. It's the trust that id has for the gamers that it's been entertaining since Carmack decided that Commander Keen grew up, joined the marines, and got deployed to Mars to fix the UAC's mistake.

But, having said that, Doom Eternal is not a game without complaints.

I spent the first half of my playthrough convinced that id and Bethesda had lost their minds. The heavy emphasis on arenas. The resource-loop economy that herds you into a predetermined battle method if you don't want to scrounge for ammo mid-fight. The story drifting further and further from the series' founding mythos. None of this is Doom 2016. Doom 2016 was a shooter in a megacorp facility with attached lore. Doom Eternal is an aim-trainer with a story attached, much the same way a male anglerfish is attached to the female, fused on for reproductive convenience, vestigial, and visibly out of place. As Shroud put it: "[Doom Eternal] It's the best aim trainer." Which is to say, the game you [i]thought[/i] you were buying is the game you had already played. The game in front of you is a [b]different[/b] game, optimized for a different player experience, and graded against its own ambition rather than the predecessor's.

That's a taste objection, not a rejection; and taste objections grounded in game structure are legitimate. 

"I prefer Doom 2016" is a sentence a sane person can write. 

"Doom Eternal is bad because I prefer Doom 2016" is not. 

And the inability to tell those two sentences apart is, I am increasingly convinced, the single defining cognitive failure of the Steam gaming-reviewer ecosystem.

Which brings us to "Doc" Burford.

Burford's Medium[dot]com piece on Doom Eternal is the load-bearing citation in roughly half the negative Steam reviews of this game. It also reads like a film-studies undergraduate trying to extract a dissertation chapter out of being bad at fighting a [i]single[/i] enemy - the Marauder. The lexicon screams "auteur theory", but what he actually means is "I died a lot and didn't enjoy dying." If your critical foundation involves reaching for "auteur theory" to dismiss a game made by a five-hundred-person studio because you specifically cannot beat one miniboss, you are not doing criticism. You are Clark from [i]Good Will Hunting[/i]. You are buying the Nikola Tesla poster of game writing and hanging it next to your Flying Spaghetti Monster print (both of which you got from that idiot at [i]The Oatmeal[/i]) and telling people who don't know any better that this is what depth looks like.

And then, having done all that, Burford turns around and calls Destiny 2 the best game ever made. The Destiny 2 of "Eververse," of the gear sunsetting catastrophe, of the season-pass treadmill that consumes the franchise's good will (and the players' actual money) in real time. Yes, [i]that[/i] Destiny 2.

Pro-tip: If the theory you've imported from Burford into your review is the same theory that rates Destiny 2 above Doom Eternal, the problem is not Doom Eternal or Destiny 2. The problem is the theory. And that theory was downloaded from a Patreon mule with three followers and a cropped headshot. Get a different theory, or try playing the game without writing the review first.

Well, this was surprising and a complete disappointment for me.

I'd go as far as to say this is single-handedly the most overrated and worst DOOM game I've played.
I've played 1, 2, 3, the 2016 reboot, and this. The 2016 reboot surprisingly flushes this one down the toilet. And I was at least able to complete 3, despite it not even really being a DOOM game.

In terms of positives, it's the usual modern DOOM things such as beautiful graphics, good musical storytelling, and decent environments. 

The main issues are glaringly obvious upon the first hour of gameplay. It's usually in the combat.

1. This game isn't a DOOM game like how 3 also wasn't a DOOM game. This game is a RPS (Rock Paper Scissors) and Resource Management game where you are fighting against the mechanics laid out before you rather than fighting against the monsters themselves. You are at constant war with the Ammo system, the Armor system, the 2 grenades you get, and the Chainsaw mechanic. This makes the game not necessarily more difficult, but instead more annoying and abrasive to deal with. On top of all of that, this still has the basically mandatory glory-kill system. You know, the worst part about 2016. Complete fail.

2. This is a tiring game, even more than Ultrakill (one of my favorite boomer-styled shooters). There is way too much going on in this game, in a bad way, with all of the upgrades, secrets, arenas, terrible "lore", and mechanics. It just straight up misses the point and charm of 2016. It's like they took all of the bad parts of the modern DOOM experience and quadrupled it. 

3. Climbing and movement options are clunky and poorly designed into the spaces of the levels. They really do make levels feel extremely linear. It's a good mechanic on paper, but in practice it's way too forced.

4. There is 0 freedom in the level design. "Secrets" and the bonus arenas are actually mandatory. The buffs and upgrades they all give you are way too valuable to actually warrant skipping them. This is bad for many reasons, but the main reason is that it creates a jarring gameplay experience where you go from frenetically dealing with the Ammo system in arenas, to exploring weirdly placed secrets. Again, tiring. 

5. There is 0 freedom in the combat experience. This is what really breaks the deal, in my opinion. DOOM is known for allowing you to play in the way that you, as the player, WANTS to play it. This game breaks this entirely by introducing RPS-style mechanics where specific weapons are used to break "weak-spots" to make kills more "efficient". This is so anti-DOOM that it's almost insane that people actually like this as a DOOM game to me.

It's not hard mechanically, like Ultrakill is, it's just tedious more than anything. This game feels like a miscalculation on all fronts. Too much focus on artificial difficulty, resource management, and, funnily of all, story and lore (in a DOOM game???).

I cannot recommend playing this game unless on HEAVY sale. Just play 2016 or boot up 2 with the Brutal DOOM mod for an actually fun experience.

Negative 2.5/5.

This game is such a downgrade from 2016.  Esp the cartoonish aspects. But you want to know what makes me madder than ****? The fact that the button for blood punch is the same as glory kill, and the game is not smart enough to know when I want to use which one. So I waste a blood punch on what was intended to be a glory kill. It's FAR from the only issue, but it's fresh on my mind. Wanna guess why? Make another Doom like 2016, ID.

A direct downgrade from Doom 2016 in nearly every way.

I was genuinely keen to finally get around to buying and playing this after finishing Doom 2016. I expected something fairly similar in terms of the menus, UI, and overall presentation. Instead, what I got was a bloated mess packed with corporate rubbish constantly trying to shove Bethesda’s branding and online nonsense in your face.

One of the most irritating parts is that the game forces you to make a Bethesda account before you can even properly start playing. Doom 2016 just threw you straight into the action without any of this corporate account nonsense getting in the way. They care more about pushing their ♥♥♥♥ down your throat than actually letting people enjoy the game.

When I launched the game for the first time the audio was completely stuffed and I had to spend about 15 minutes messing around with settings just to get sound working properly. After finally fixing that, I loaded into my save and the intro cinematic got skipped because it "wasn't a new save". I paused the game to go back to the main menu and could not find the option anywhere at all.

The whole experience felt scuffed and frustrating before I’d even properly started playing. Absolute ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ of a game. Refunded.
